# ANALYSIS: BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON AM vs PM Quality Split

**Filed:** 2026-05-24
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** quality_gate (time-of-day analysis)
**Status:** DRAFT (investigation)

## Hypothesis

BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON signals in v14_enhanced watcher may exhibit time-of-day quality bias. J's winning anchor days (4/29 13:04 ET, 5/01 13:09 ET, 5/04) all occurred in the PM session (12:00-16:00 ET). If AM signals underperform PM, excluding AM entries could improve OP-16 edge_capture.

## Data Source

Using `automation/state/watcher-observations.jsonl` — v14_enhanced watcher observations with BEARISH direction.

## Method

1. Filter v14_enhanced observations to `direction="bear"` (BEARISH_REJECTION setup)
2. Extract bar_timestamp, classify as AM (09:30-12:00 ET) or PM (12:00-16:00 ET)
3. Apply L67 dedup: unique by `bar_timestamp[:16]` (minute-level dedup)
4. Compute WR and avg P&L per session
5. Map to J anchor days to assess edge_capture impact

## Expected J-Anchor Coverage

| J day | Time (ET) | Session | Current engine behavior | Proposed behavior |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | 13:04 | PM | Takes (BEARISH_REJECTION) | PM → keeps |
| 5/01 winner | 13:09 | PM | Takes (BEARISH_REJECTION) | PM → keeps |
| 5/04 winner | N/A-sniper | N/A | SNIPER (different class) | N/A |
| 5/05 loser | TBD | AM? | Skips (guard) | AM excluded → keeps skip |
| 5/06 loser | TBD | AM? | Skips (guard) | AM excluded → keeps skip |
| 5/07 loser 1 | TBD | PM | Skips (guard) | PM → keeps skip |
| 5/07 loser 2 | TBD | PM | Skips (guard) | PM → keeps skip |

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** N/A (watcher-only analysis)
2. **Sample bias:** v14_enhanced watcher observations — depends on watcher firing criteria
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (time-of-day split requires walk-forward validation)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top-3 J days need fill validation)
5. **Failure modes:** If AM signals are actually profitable on non-J days, excluding them loses edge
6. **Concentration:** Unknown — requires data extraction

## Pre-merge gate

- Extract watcher-observations.jsonl data
- Compute AM vs PM split with L67 dedup
- Validate J-anchor time classification
- Walk-forward test (if data supports)

## Confidence

3 / 10 — **Requires data extraction first.** The hypothesis is plausible (PM concentration in J winners), but need concrete numbers before proposing a gate.

## Next Steps

1. Load `automation/state/watcher-observations.jsonl`
2. Filter to v14_enhanced + bear direction
3. Parse timestamps, classify AM/PM
4. Apply L67 dedup, compute WR/P&L per session
5. Map to J anchor days
6. If AM underperforms significantly, propose PM-only gate candidate
